params.text:
Cleaning, decontamination and common infections

Cleaning and decontamination

A clean environment reduces the germs a person is exposed to. Clean frequently touched surfaces - door handles, taps, grab rails, commodes - regularly with the right product, following manufacturer and local guidance. Use the principle of clean, then disinfect where needed, and follow "clean to dirty" so you do not spread contamination. Use single-use equipment where possible, or decontaminate shared equipment between people. Colour-coded cloths help keep, for example, toilet cleaning separate from kitchen cleaning.

Healthcare-associated infections (HCAIs) to know

  • MRSA - a bacterium resistant to some antibiotics. Many people carry it harmlessly on the skin, but it can cause serious infection if it enters a wound or device. Hand hygiene and wound care are key.
  • C. difficile - causes severe diarrhoea, often after antibiotics. It forms tough spores; use soap and water for hands and a chlorine-based cleaner for surfaces.
  • Norovirus - the "winter vomiting bug": sudden vomiting and diarrhoea, spreads very easily. Soap and water for hands; isolate cases; strict cleaning.
  • Influenza and COVID-19 - respiratory viruses spread by droplets. Respiratory hygiene, PPE, ventilation and vaccination reduce spread.

params.title: Tap a card to flip it
params.description: Learn the key facts about four common healthcare-associated infections.

dialogs.answer: A bacterium resistant to some antibiotics. Often carried harmlessly on skin, but dangerous if it enters a wound or medical device. Control with hand hygiene, wound care and screening.
Causes severe diarrhoea, often after antibiotics. Forms spores that alcohol gel cannot kill - wash hands with soap and water and use a chlorine-based cleaner on surfaces.
The 'winter vomiting bug' - sudden vomiting and diarrhoea that spreads very easily. Use soap and water, isolate affected people, and clean thoroughly. Report clusters as a possible outbreak.
Respiratory viruses spread by droplets and close contact. Reduce spread with respiratory hygiene, fluid-resistant masks, good ventilation and vaccination of staff and residents.
